close_reason: Style: redundant closure vs method reference — marginal
description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:82.

Current code:
    let lib_path = parsed
        .param("lib")
        .map(|s| s.to_string())
        .or_else(|| std::env::var("PKCS11_LIB").ok())
        ...

The closure |s| s.to_string() can be replaced by str::to_string or ToString::to_string. clippy::redundant_closure flags this in pedantic mode.

Idiomatic:
    let lib_path = parsed
        .param("lib")
        .map(str::to_string)
        .or_else(|| std::env::var("PKCS11_LIB").ok())
        ...

Why it matters: the closure version reads as if there's a transformation step; the function-pointer version makes "convert &str to String" explicit. Tiny win, but it's the kind of thing pedantic Rust readers notice.

Defensible counter-argument: |s| s.to_string() is more searchable when grepping for to_string call sites. If you weight searchability over conciseness, leave it. Mark this as opinion.
title: Redundant closure |s| s.to_string() at line 82
